title: Cyathea melleri
taxonomy: Cyathea melleri is classified as a species of tree fern in the family Cyatheaceae. It is a part of the genus Cyathea, which belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta. The family Cyatheaceae is also known as the scaly tree ferns. Cyathea melleri is native to New Zealand and has been classified as endangered due to habitat loss. The species is further classified as Cyathea melleri, which is a taxonomic binomial nomenclature used to identify it uniquely.

However, some research suggests that tree ferns in general, including Cyathea melleri, have been used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
